summary: Vertical Lines in Distal Esophageal Mucosa (VLEM) are actually manifestations of dilated esophageal veins, specifically related to venous congestion and structural changes from conditions like portal hypertension or post-procedural states—not inflammatory processes. Thus, they represent an alternative diagnosis distinct from pediatric esophagitis.
